appeal_letter:
To the Cigna Appeals Department:

I am appealing the denial of coverage for Semaglutide (Wegovy) as prescribed by my treating provider.

The denial states two grounds. First, that my employer-sponsored pharmacy benefit plan has a strict exclusion for all weight-loss medications. Second, that I do not have a diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes, which the letter states would be required for coverage of similar GLP-1 medications under the medical benefit. On these grounds the request was denied as a plan exclusion and as not medically necessary.

The basis for this appeal is that the criterion relied upon does not fit the documented clinical purpose of this prescription. According to my clinical record, my provider did not prescribe Wegovy for weight loss. The medication was prescribed to prevent the onset of Type 2 Diabetes in a patient who is on the verge of developing it. The intended and documented use is preventative treatment of an impending metabolic disease, not cosmetic or general weight reduction. Applied to these documented facts, the requested medication is being used for a medical purpose that the weight-loss-drug exclusion does not address.

For this reason I ask that the request be reviewed on its individual medical merits rather than disposed of solely under the weight-loss-medication exclusion. The denial treats the prescription as a weight-loss drug, but the documented clinical intent is prevention of Type 2 Diabetes. Where the plan's general exclusion does not contemplate this preventative medical use, I ask that an individualized medical-necessity determination be made on the documented facts of my case.

I further note that the denial letter does not state the deadline for filing this appeal or describe my further appeal rights, including any right to an external or independent review. I request that Cigna provide that information in writing.

Requested action: I ask that Cigna overturn the denial and approve coverage of Semaglutide (Wegovy), or in the alternative conduct an individualized medical-necessity review of the prescription as a preventative treatment for the onset of Type 2 Diabetes, and that Cigna provide the appeal deadline and a full description of my remaining appeal rights.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Respectfully,
Member
missing_evidence_checklist: Provider letter of medical necessity stating that Wegovy is prescribed to prevent the onset of Type 2 Diabetes rather than for weight loss
Clinical documentation of prediabetes or impending Type 2 Diabetes (for example lab values such as HbA1c or fasting glucose) supporting the preventative diagnosis
The specific plan language of the weight-loss-medication exclusion and any exception or medical-necessity review process it allows
Documentation of any prior treatments tried for weight or metabolic risk and their outcomes
The plan's stated appeal deadline and description of internal and external appeal rights
